evidence: "Damn this was a tense movie. More a suspense or thriller than a horror film... One of the best psychological thrillers I've seen in a long time."
"I know this grief personally and it is worse than death, death would have been more merciful."
"I did not like the second storyline about the towns people, it seemed unnecessary and confusing..."
"The kind of film that gets in your head."
"I thought the pace of the film was perfect. I don't think it was too slow or that there were any unnecessary scenes."
"This movie was sooooo boring it was painful."
"I love this film. I find the premise very disturbing. Beginning to end was tension."
"I didn't find it confusing or convoluted, it is all pretty obvious who's who."
"I only watched this because it was written by the writer of Beasts of the Southern Wild..."
"Usually love Ozon movies but this one was awful."
"You have to watch this with the sound up and no distractions..."
"So beautiful it's painful. A very intense and real character study."
"What moved me most was the ending..."
"I watched this on Mothers day, it's how I cope. I watch and read everything I can find on adoption and cry all day."

summary: The user has a strong preference for psychological thrillers and dramas, often appreciating complex narratives and emotional depth. They value high production quality and sensory experiences, while showing a tendency towards slower pacing in storytelling.
